summary: Decompiled a large slice of the Arwing Laser item implementation, adding its state table, physics/collision callbacks, spawn helpers, reflect/mirror handlers, and a more complete xDD4 item-var layout. The bot reported +2992 matched code bytes, +224 matched data bytes, 10 new 100% matches, and three newly near-matching functions.

key_files.role: Main Arwing Laser decomp work: state table, attrs, spawn helpers, physics, collision, reflect/mirror handlers.
Expanded itArwingLaser_ItemVars from padded placeholders into explicit fields through ip+0xE4C.
Exported new public prototypes and cleaned a duplicate it_802E838C declaration.

what_changed: Added ArwingLaserAttr with ItemAttr pointer plus x4/x8 float fields, and added dependencies for ftlib, ground/stage/Corneria, item collision helpers, lbvector, mplib, trig/math, and JObj operations.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F8DE8 with six Arwing Laser states: states 0/2 use UnkMotion2 anim/phys/coll, states 1/3 use UnkMotion3 anim/phys/coll, and states 4/5 use UnkMotion5_Coll only.
Added static Vec2 it_803F8E48 cardinal probe offsets of +/-200 units used by collision probing.

Implemented it_802E70BC as a line/collision probe around the laser bone position using lb_8000B1CC, it_8026EA9C, mpGetSpeed, mpLineGetFlags, and xE48 as an argument to the item collision helper; bot reported it at 90.59%, not fully matched.

Implemented it_802E72E0 as an Arwing Laser spawn helper for type 0/2 and child type 4/5 lasers, initializing SpawnItem kind 0xEA, bone/world position, parent and bone links, scale fields, lifetime, JObj rotation, and recursive child side-beam objects; bot reported it at 96.95%.

Implemented it_802E7654 as a directed laser spawn helper from a bone toward a target Vec3, normalizing xE0C direction, deriving facing_dir, setting JObj rotations with atan2f, calling it_802E7A4C/it_802750F8, and storing owner/type/arg4/scale state; bot reported it at 98.00%.

Implemented matched physics callbacks itArwinglaser_UnkMotion2_Phys and itArwinglaser_UnkMotion3_Phys, including bone-following before launch, throw-speed velocity, scale from Ground_801C0498 and xE34, attrs->x8 scale stretching, and Corneria stage offset handling through Stage_8022519C/Stage_80225194/grCorneria_801DDCF0.

Implemented matched collision callbacks itArwinglaser_UnkMotion2_Coll, itArwinglaser_UnkMotion3_Coll, and itArwinglaser_UnkMotion5_Coll, covering previous/current bone positions, z-flattened collision checks, effects via efSync_Spawn(0x40E), sound/effect calls through Item_8026AE84, first-hit xE4C logic, and child laser transform synchronization to the parent beam.

Implemented matched reflect/mirror handlers it_802E8420 and it_802E85F4, setting xE3C, updating owner/reflect gobj context, reversing facing/velocity for type 0/2, using ftLib_800865C0 for reflected directed lasers, and lbVector_Mirror for shield/ground-style mirroring.

Expanded itArwingLaser_ItemVars to include xDF8 HSD_JObj*, xDFC/xE00/xE04/xE08 floats, xE0C direction Vec3, xE34 scale float, xE3C flag/state, xE48 argument field, and xE4C counter, replacing prior padding around these offsets.
smart_moves: The item-var header work made the code readable and matchable by replacing broad padding with explicit offset fields while preserving placeholder offset names where semantics were not proven.
The state table was reconstructed alongside the code, yielding data matches and documenting how the same anim/phys/coll pairs are reused for paired states 0/2, 1/3, and child states 4/5.
The spawn helpers carefully preserve parent-child relationships: type 0 creates hidden/attached type 4 and 5 child lasers, records xE40/xE44, and uses signed z handling for the side beams.

The physics code accounts for moving stage behavior by applying Corneria offsets only for stage ID 14 and falling through/returning for stage ID 15, a detail that likely matters for both behavior and matching.

The collision implementation reuses existing item and map primitives rather than inventing new logic: it_8026EA20/it_8026EA9C for line checks, mpGetSpeed for surface velocity, mpLineGetFlags to filter lines, and Item_8026AE84/efSync_Spawn for hit feedback.
decomp_lessons: For item decomp, establish the xDD4 item-var layout early; even placeholder offset fields are better than padding when multiple callbacks share state across spawn, physics, collision, reflection, and child objects.
State tables and small static data arrays can produce clean data/sdata/sdata2 matches even when large spawn helpers remain only near-matched.

Item spawn functions often need exact SpawnItem initialization order, explicit velocity zeroing, parent/bone fields, lifetime calls like it_80275158, and immediate state setup in xDD4_itemVar before motion-state entry.

Arwing Laser behavior is tightly coupled to bone world positions from lb_8000B1CC; future similar item work should track previous/current bone endpoints and intentionally flatten z when the collision is planar.
Stage-specific offsets and moving-ground helpers can be required inside item physics, not just stage code; check gr/stage dependencies when an item appears to drift with stage geometry.
No human review rationale was recorded, so lessons about intent are inferred from the diff and decomp-dev matching report rather than reviewer comments.
naming_conventions: Static callbacks follow the existing placeholder pattern itArwinglaser_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll, with the exact mixed-case spelling `itArwinglaser`.
Public functions remain address-based names such as it_802E70BC, it_802E72E0, it_802E7654, it_802E8420, and it_802E85F4 until semantics are established.
Struct fields in itArwingLaser_ItemVars keep offset-style names xDF4 through xE4C, with comments showing both struct-relative offsets and absolute `ip+0x...` item offsets.
Special-attribute fields are locally named ArwingLaserAttr with placeholder members x0, x4, and x8 because the diff does not prove semantic names.
Header cleanup removed a duplicate it_802E838C prototype and kept exports in numeric-address order around the existing declarations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: State table entries cast callback functions to HSD_GObjPredicate and HSD_GObjEvent, matching the expected ItemStateTable function-pointer types.
The code preserves matching-oriented temporaries and pads such as unused float locals in itArwinglaser_UnkMotion2_Phys plus `u32 pad` and `u8 pad` locals in other functions.
Rotation Y for horizontal beams uses `(f32) (1.5707963267948966 * (f64) ip->facing_dir)`, preserving the double constant and cast pattern rather than simplifying to a float macro.
Vector and SpawnItem initialization is written explicitly, including separate z/y/x velocity zero assignments and repeated Vec3 copies, which may be important for codegen.
Collision checks intentionally zero z components before calling map/item line tests, while child beam spawn types 4/5 preserve mirrored signed z values.
The Corneria stage branch uses a switch with `case 14` applying grCorneria_801DDCF0 offsets and falling through to `case 15: return;`, an exact control-flow detail worth reusing for matching.

it_802E8420 distinguishes owner versus xC64_reflectGObj using xDCC_flag.b1 before calling ftLib_800865C0, while it_802E85F4 uses lbVector_Mirror against ip->xC58; these are separate reflect/mirror idioms.
review_feedback: PR body only said: "Tracking PR as usual".
No line review comments were present.
One blank approval review was submitted by jellejurre at 2026-04-02T20:38:15Z.
decomp-dev bot reported matched code 61.58% (+0.08%, +2992 bytes) and matched data 37.83% (+0.02%, +224 bytes).

Bot listed 10 new 100% matches: itArwinglaser_UnkMotion5_Coll, it_802E8420, itArwinglaser_UnkMotion3_Phys, it_802E85F4, itArwinglaser_UnkMotion2_Phys, itArwinglaser_UnkMotion2_Coll, itArwinglaser_UnkMotion3_Coll, .data, .sdata2, and .sdata.
Bot listed three new but not fully matched functions: it_802E7654 at 98.00%, it_802E72E0 at 96.95%, and it_802E70BC at 90.59%.
follow_up_queries: Investigate remaining mismatch in it_802E70BC, which bot reported at 90.59%.
Investigate remaining mismatch in it_802E72E0, which bot reported at 96.95%.
Investigate remaining mismatch in it_802E7654, which bot reported at 98.00%.
Search callsites of it_802E72E0 and it_802E7654 to name parameters currently represented as parent, owner, bone, type, arg4, scale, and scale_mult.
Map itArwingLaser_ItemVars fields xDF4/xDF8/xDFC/xE0C/xE34/xE38/xE3C/xE48/xE4C to semantic names once more Arwing or Corneria code is decompiled.
Confirm whether ArwingLaserAttr should remain local to itarwinglaser.c or move to a shared item attribute header when neighboring article data is understood.
Look for other item collision code using it_8026EA9C with mpLineGetFlags & 0x100 to clarify the line-filter semantics.
